results.possibleIdentity: Ambuga Ranganath (also known as Rangi Ranganath) was an Indian-American engineer active in the New York technical community during the late 1960s and early 1970s. Born on March 11, 1942, in Karnataka, India, he emigrated to the United States in 1963 to pursue a Master's degree in engineering at Columbia University. He was professionally associated with Universal Unlimited Incorporated, a company based in Glen Cove and Hempstead, Long Island, that specialized in the machining and thermo-vacuum forming of plastic systems (such as Plexiglas acrylic panels). Ranganath's technical expertise spanned electronic power systems and plastics engineering. He was a member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) network, where he offered his technical services to promote collaborations between artists and engineers. In his later career, he became a self-employed energy consultant and an author, publishing several books on topics ranging from linguistics and religion to American humor. He resided in Uniondale and Glen Cove, New York, until his death on January 21, 2016.
